title: Powerloom Schema Dialect v1
description: Meta-schema defining the x-powerloom-* annotation keywords layered on top of JSON Schema Draft 2020-12. These keywords carry control-plane semantics (server-populated fields, immutability, cross-kind refs, reconciler hints, tier gating, secret resolution, auxiliary extensions, apply-order). Tooling that understands this dialect should honor the keywords; tooling that does not will treat them as inert annotations (vanilla JSON Schema behavior).

x-powerloom-ref.description: Declares that a string field is a reference to another Powerloom resource. The CLI resolves these at apply time; the API validates target existence at admit time.

kind.description: Target kind the reference resolves to.

resolve_by.description: Whether the reference string is a human-authored name/path or a server-issued ID.

scope.description: Search scope for the ref. 'ou' means the ref must resolve within the target OU subtree.

on_missing.description: Behavior when the target does not exist at apply time.

x-powerloom-server-field.description: true means the field is populated by the server (e.g. IDs, timestamps, hash chains). Client-authored manifests MUST NOT set it; the schema rejects it if present on input.

x-powerloom-immutable.description: true means the field cannot change after create. PATCH requests that attempt to change an immutable field are rejected with a specific error.

x-powerloom-reconciler-hint.description: Declares which reconciler(s) care about this field and whether a change should force a full re-reconcile or a cheap drift-only pass.

cost.description: Cheap = DB-only delta; expensive = external provider call (Anthropic, AWS, etc.).

x-powerloom-secret-ref.description: Declares that a field, once resolved, yields a secret value retrieved via the CredentialStore. Manifests author the *reference*, never the secret value itself.

x-powerloom-default-from-server.description: The server will populate this field if the client omits it, based on organization/OU policy. Distinct from x-powerloom-server-field: the client MAY set it to override the policy default (subject to RBAC).

x-powerloom-auxiliary.description: Marks the containing object as accepting auxiliary attributes from one or more AuxiliaryClass definitions (AD-style auxiliary class pattern). Lands in Phase 15.4.

storage.description: Where auxiliary attributes are persisted on the target row.

allowed_classes.description: If set, restricts which AuxiliaryClass names may extend this kind. Empty = any.

x-powerloom-example.description: A minimal, valid, agent-demo-grade example payload for this field or kind. Powers the luddite-demo target: Claude sessions should be able to pattern-match off these.

x-powerloom-tier-availability.description: Which tiers may use this field/kind/feature. Absent = all tiers.

cap_metric.description: Name of the quota metric enforced by Phase 15 middleware (e.g. 'agents_per_org', 'workflow_runs_per_month').

x-powerloom-apply-order.description: Hint to the CLI planner for ordering apply operations across kinds. Lower = earlier. Typical values: OU=10, Group=20, RoleBinding=30, Credential=40, Skill=50, MCPServerRegistration=60, MCPDeployment=70, Agent=80, AgentSkill=90, AgentMCPServer=95, Workflow=100.
